acquisition_type: NON-IT Goods.note: Acquisition type describes purchased input, not government function. Requires department, program, commodity, or item context before COFOG assignment.

acquisition_type: IT Goods.note: IT goods can support any function. Do not classify as COFOG 01 merely because it is administrative technology.

acquisition_type: NON-IT Services.note: Service procurement is an input category. Function depends on department/program/purpose.

acquisition_type: IT Services.note: IT services can support any function. Requires joined context.

acquisition_type: IT Telecommunications.note: Telecommunications procurement is not itself a government function. Requires joined context.

department: Corrections and Rehabilitati.note: Corrections/prisons align with public order and safety. Healthcare inside corrections may need secondary health tags but department function is public order/safety.

department: Consumer Affairs, Department.note: Regulatory/general administration function inferred from department name only. Needs program-level confirmation.

department: Correctional Health Care Ser.note: Health-care delivery function is explicit in source department name, though institutional setting is corrections.

department: Transportation, Department o.note: Transportation aligns with economic affairs / transport.

department: Water Resources, Department.note: Water resources may span housing/community amenities, environmental protection, and economic affairs. Department-level mapping is weak.

department: Fish and Wildlife, Departmen.note: Wildlife conservation aligns with environmental protection.

department: Forestry and Fire Protection.note: Fire protection aligns with public order/safety, while forestry/natural-resource work can align with environmental protection. Needs program split.

department: State Hospitals, Department.note: Hospital/health function inferred from department name.

department: Veterans Affairs, Department.note: Veterans services can include social protection, health, housing, and memorial functions. Department-level mapping is weak; program split needed.

department: Franchise Tax Board.note: Tax administration aligns with general public services.

department: Parks & Recreation, Departme.note: Parks/recreation aligns with recreation, culture and religion.

department: Industrial Relations, Depart.note: Labor/industrial regulation inferred as economic affairs, but department programs may include public safety and social protection components.

department: Developmental Services, Depa.note: Developmental disability services align primarily with social protection; health components may need secondary tagging.

department: Water Resources Control Boar.note: Water quality/regulation aligns with environmental protection.

department: Pesticide Regulation, Depart.note: Pesticide regulation has environmental/public-health/agricultural aspects. Environmental protection is a tentative department-level assignment.

department: Conservation Corps, Californ.note: Conservation suggests environmental protection, but youth/employment components may fall under social protection or economic affairs.

department: Military Department.note: Military function suggests defence, but state emergency/disaster missions may map to public order/safety. Program split needed.

department: Air Resources Board.note: Air-quality regulation aligns with environmental protection.

department: Health & Human Services Agen.note: Agency spans health and social protection. Department-level mapping would obscure real split.

department: Highway Patrol, California.note: Police/highway patrol aligns with public order and safety.

department: Motor Vehicles, Department o.note: Transport administration aligns with economic affairs, but some enforcement/identity functions may differ.

department: Employment Development Depar.note: Unemployment/social insurance functions suggest social protection, while labor-market programs may map to economic affairs. Program split needed.
